mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.5pp, or t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid contraction in industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and falling corporate output, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of high-inflation months, or inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months with positive payroll growth, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real GDP growth combined with declining real personal consumption expenditures confirms a demand shock that reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Rebound in industrial production following a supply disruption signals restored manufacturing capacity and improved earnings expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rebound instances, or the signal performs no better than random during recovery phases.
